title: Quiz: Key Milestones in PQC Standardization

questions.question: When did NIST issue the official call for PQC proposals?
questions.explanation: NIST issued the official call for post-quantum cryptographic algorithm proposals on December 20, 2016.

questions.question: How many algorithms advanced to Round 2 of the NIST PQC competition?
questions.explanation: 26 algorithms advanced to Round 2 in January 2019, down from 69 candidates in Round 1.

questions.question: When were the final FIPS standards (203, 204, 205) officially published?
questions.explanation: The final FIPS 203 (ML-KEM), FIPS 204 (ML-DSA), and FIPS 205 (SLH-DSA) standards were officially published on August 13, 2024.

questions.question: Which two algorithms were broken in 2022?
questions.explanation: In 2022, both Rainbow (multivariate signature, broken by Beullens) and SIKE (isogeny-based KEM, broken by Castryck-Decru) were completely broken.

questions.question: How many finalists and alternates were announced for Round 3?
questions.explanation: Round 3 announced 7 finalists (Kyber, NTRU, SABER, Classic McEliece, Dilithium, FALCON, Rainbow) and 8 alternates.

questions.question: What is the 'Harvest Now, Decrypt Later' threat?

options.text: Capturing encrypted data now to decrypt when quantum computers exist

questions.explanation: 'Harvest Now, Decrypt Later' refers to adversaries capturing encrypted data today and storing it until quantum computers capable of breaking the encryption become available.

questions.question: The NIST PQC standardization process took approximately 4 years from call to final standards.
questions.explanation: The process took approximately 8 years - from the December 2016 call for proposals to the August 2024 publication of final standards.
